title: Implement 3D point cloud base geometry
summary: Create a new GeometrySystem that renders a Points-based 3D point cloud with procedural volumetric shape, non-zero Z-depth, camera drift for parallax, and quality-tier-scaled point count. Register it in the ModeManager alongside existing particle and wave systems.

steps.title: Add maxPoints to QualityProfile
steps.description: Add a `maxPoints` field to `QualityProfile` interface and set tier-specific values: low=200, medium=500, high=1200. These are separate from `maxParticles` because the point cloud is a denser volumetric structure requiring different budget.
changes: Set maxPoints in each tier return: low=200, medium=500, high=1200

steps.title: Create pointCloud.ts geometry system
steps.description: Implement a GeometrySystem that generates a 3D point cloud with visible depth using THREE.Points and BufferGeometry. The cloud shape is procedurally generated from the session seed using the existing PRNG.

init_method: Create PRNG from seed + ':pointcloud' suffix for deterministic generation
Compute effectiveCount from density × maxPoints × (0.6 + structureComplexity × 0.4) — same scaling formula as particleField

Generate 3D positions using a seeded procedural volume shape. Use a mix of shell and interior sampling: for each point, pick a random shape (sphere shell, inner volume, or lattice-perturbed grid) weighted by structureComplexity. curveSoftness controls interpolation between angular (lattice) and smooth (sphere) shapes
Z-depth range: points span ±2.5 units in Z (compared to particleField's ±1), ensuring non-flat geometry
Create Float32Array for positions (×3), colors (×3), and sizes (×1)
Store basePositions copy for displacement calculations
Create THREE.BufferGeometry with position, color, size attributes
Create THREE.PointsMaterial with vertexColors, sizeAttenuation, transparent, blending: AdditiveBlending for glow effect

shape_generation.description: Procedural volume from seed — no external models
shape_generation.approach: Hybrid sphere-shell + volume fill. For each point: rng() < 0.6 → shell point (radius 2 ± 0.3 perturbation), rng() >= 0.6 → interior volume point (random radius 0-1.8). All coordinates use spherical→cartesian conversion with seeded theta/phi. structureComplexity > 0.7 adds lattice-aligned snapping for crystalline feel.
shape_generation.z_depth: Spherical generation naturally produces full Z range. Minimum Z spread enforced at ±1.5 even at lowest complexity.

draw_method: Apply bass-driven macro rotation: rotate entire cloud slowly around Y axis, amplitude scaled by bassEnergy × motionAmplitude
Apply treble-driven per-point jitter: high-frequency displacement scaled by trebleEnergy × motionAmplitude
Apply pointer disturbance: points near normalized pointer position get pushed outward (radial repulsion from pointer ray)
Apply time-based breathing: gentle scale oscillation (sine wave on elapsed) for organic feel
Update colors from paletteHue with per-point hueOffset, sparkle modulation on treble peaks
Set needsUpdate on position and color attributes
camera_drift: In draw(), apply a subtle camera-like effect by rotating the Points mesh around Y axis with a slow sine/cosine oscillation (period ~20s)
This creates parallax — near points move more than far points in screen space, making depth perceptible
Additionally add a small Z-axis oscillation (±0.3) for depth breathing

steps.title: Register pointCloud in ModeManager
steps.description: Import createPointCloud, instantiate it with quality-driven config, and add it as a third mode entry in the ModeManager.
changes: After waves creation, add: `const pointCloud = createPointCloud({ maxPoints: quality.maxPoints, enableSparkle: quality.enableSparkle })`
Add `{ name: 'pointcloud', factory: () => pointCloud }` to the ModeManager modes array

steps.title: Write tests for pointCloud system
steps.description: Follow the same testing patterns as particleField.test.ts. Tests validate all acceptance criteria.

tests.description: init() adds a THREE.Points mesh to the scene
tests.validates: AC: scene contains Points-based 3D point cloud

tests.description: Points mesh uses BufferGeometry with position attribute (itemSize 3)
Point positions have non-zero Z-depth: Z values span a range > 1.0
tests.validates: AC: non-zero Z-depth, not a flat plane

tests.description: No point has Z === 0 for all points (cloud is not coplanar with XY plane)
Mesh rotation changes over elapsed time (camera drift / parallax)
tests.validates: AC: depth perceptible via parallax or camera drift

tests.description: Point count with maxPoints=200 produces fewer points than maxPoints=1200
tests.validates: AC: point count scales by quality tier

tests.description: Point count with maxPoints config never exceeds the configured maximum
tests.validates: AC: point count scales by quality tier

tests.description: Same seed produces identical initial positions (deterministic)
Different seeds produce different initial positions
draw() does not throw with valid FrameState
draw() flags position buffer needsUpdate (version increments)
cleanup() removes mesh from scene and disposes geometry/material
Bass energy influences point cloud motion (positions differ with bass=0 vs bass=1)
Treble energy influences point jitter
Colors derive from paletteHue (different hue produces different color buffer)
Has color buffer attribute with vertexColors enabled on material
No localStorage or cookie access during init/draw operations
Density parameter scales effective point count (higher density = more points)
Low motionAmplitude produces less displacement than high motionAmplitude
Boundary values (density=0, density=1, structureComplexity=0, structureComplexity=1) do not throw

implementation_order: Step 5: Run tests, fix issues, verify build passes

key_patterns_to_follow.geometry_system_interface: Implement GeometrySystem { init, draw, cleanup } from src/visual/types.ts
key_patterns_to_follow.buffer_geometry: Use Float32Array + BufferAttribute, update via needsUpdate flag — no per-frame object creation
key_patterns_to_follow.test_structure: Mirror particleField.test.ts patterns: shared defaultParams, scene-per-test, spy-based privacy checks

risks.risk: Camera drift via mesh rotation may feel different from true camera movement
risks.mitigation: Mesh rotation around Y-axis produces equivalent parallax effect to camera orbit. If needed, can switch to actual camera position oscillation in renderLoop, but mesh rotation is self-contained within the GeometrySystem interface.

risks.risk: High point counts on mobile could hurt performance
risks.mitigation: Quality tier system already in place: low tier gets 200 points, well within mobile budgets. sizeAttenuation and additive blending are GPU-friendly.
